title: Updates are shown to viewers if they are mentioned or associated with the update box
excerpt: Hey team, how can I have updates notify the viewers as well, if they are associated with that particular update or task. Viewers cannot see members task, but if they are mentioned in one of the box as referral, I want th…

posts.author: abhishek007dhiman (Abhishek Dhiman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey team, how can I have updates notify the viewers as well, if they are associated with that particular update or task. Viewers cannot see members task, but if they are mentioned in one of the box as referral, I want the update to also notify them of the changes being made. Please let me know what can be done. Thanks

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
To notify viewers in Monday.com about task updates, you can mention them directly in updates using @, which triggers a notification. Additionally, set up automations to notify viewers based on changes to relevant tasks or columns, ensuring they stay informed even without direct task access.

In case you need any help setting it up let me know here and I’ll be happy to assist.

Zaki

Business Workflow Consultant

Ready Logic

posts.author: MaxKP (Max)
posts.cookedHtml:
@ZakiImtiaz I saw your rely here but am unable to replicate this within the Emails & Activities section – would be great if you could shed some light if what I’m trying to do is impossible:

  1. Create a custom activity with a template (say, like a meeting).
  2. The template draws from a person column, and that column can or can not be populated by a “viewer” user.

→ All I get is automation email notifications on my own Monday account, despite turning these off (!), but the viewers whom I actually tag get no notification at all: not in-app, not via email.
